question: What percentage of the vote will Nigel Farage receive in the Clacton parliamentary by-election in August 2026?

factors.description: All five major parties boycott; ~54% of the previously-split serious opposition vote removed from a strong Leave/Reform seat
12-15 mostly novelty/independent candidates split the anti-Farage vote, no credible single challenger to consolidate opposition
Differential turnout favors energized Reform base; Reform national polling 25-27% vs 14% in 2024
Downside: intense national anti-Farage sentiment could mobilize protest turnout for Binface (Bercow 2010 held boycotted incumbent near 47%)

summary: Farage received 46.2% in Clacton in the 2024 GE against full competition (Con 27.9%, Lab 16.2%, others). For this by-election, all major parties (Con, Lab, LibDem, Green, Restore Britain) are boycotting, leaving only novelty/independent candidates (Count Binface as nominal "main challenger") across a 12-15 candidate field that splits the residual anti-Farage vote many ways. Reform is nationally much stronger now (25-27% vs 14% in 2024), Clacton is a strong Leave/Reform seat, and differential turnout in a boycotted "vanity" contest favors the energized Reform base. This lifts Farage's share well above his 2024 baseline. Center ~64%. Downside risk: intense national anti-Farage sentiment (33% prefer Binface, 52% say don't re-elect) could mobilize protest turnout and consolidate on Binface (Bercow 2010 precedent held a boycotted incumbent near 47%). No constituency-level poll exists, so genuine uncertainty is wide. Scenario-mixture Monte Carlo (55% base ~64, 25% landslide ~73, 20% embarrassment ~55).
reasoning:
Forecast submitted. 

**Summary:** For the Clacton by-election (13 Aug 2026), I forecast Farage's Reform UK vote share centered at **~64%** (P10 ~54.5%, P50 ~64.4%, P90 ~74.4%).

**Key reasoning:**
- Farage won 46.2% in 2024 against full competition. Now all five major parties (Conservative, Labour, Lib Dem, Green, Restore Britain) are boycotting, removing ~54% of the previously-split serious opposition.
- The remaining field is 12-15 novelty/independent candidates (Count Binface as nominal "main challenger"), which fragments the anti-Farage vote and inflates his percentage.
- Reform is nationally much stronger now (25-27% vs 14% in 2024), Clacton is a strong Leave/Reform seat, and differential turnout in a boycotted contest favors his energized base.
- Downside risk (kept the lower tail wide): intense national anti-Farage sentiment could mobilize protest turnout for Binface — the Bercow 2010 precedent shows a boycotted incumbent can be held near 47%. No constituency-level poll exists, so uncertainty is genuinely wide.

The premortem reviewer approved the forecast.
